🌱 Add rook: CephBlockPoolRadosNamespace Force Deletion Blocks Watcher mission (#1793)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "rook-16480-cephblockpoolradosnamespace-force-deletion-blocks-watcher-ip-without-",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "rook: CephBlockPoolRadosNamespace Force Deletion Blocks Watcher IP without Specifying Session ID",
+    "description": "CephBlockPoolRadosNamespace Force Deletion Blocks Watcher IP without Specifying Session ID. This issue affects 9+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify rook troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your rook deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook -n rook --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review rook configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant rook configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook\nkubectl get configmap -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rook\n```\n**Is this a bug report or feature request?**\n* Bug Report\n\n**Deviation from expected behavior:**\nIn https://github.com/rook/rook/pull/16009, a feature was added that blocks the watcher IP of each image before moving it to trash to ensure Ceph will"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for CephBlockPoolRadosNamespace Force Deletion Blocks Watcher…",
+        "description": "blocklisting IP can cause problems when the cleanup job pod runs on the same host or gets the same ip as that of the rbd client ip that is present in the rbd status/watchers. instead of blocklisting the whole ip of the node or the client blocklist\nonly the unique ip:nonce to allow the node to not\n```yaml\n2025-09-11 16:37:31.434476 D | exec: Running command: ceph rbd task add trash remove my-pool/my-namespace/cf64122559b990 --connect-timeout=15 --cluster=rook-ceph --conf=/var/lib/rook/rook-ceph/rook-ceph.config --name=client.admin --keyring=/var/lib/rook/rook-ceph/client.admin.keyring --format json\n2025-09-11 16:37:31.785810 E | cleanup: failed to add task to remove image \"csi-vol-456f3d06-cbf2-44ee-bb7b-c3fa01d3a884\" from trash in cephblockpool \"my-pool\" in rados namespace \"my-namespace\": failed to delete image \"cf64122559b990\" in cephblockpool \"my-pool\" from trash: exit status 11\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm CephBlockPoolRadosNamespace Force Deletion Blocks… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook -n rook --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n rook --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "blocklisting IP can cause problems when the cleanup job pod runs on the same host or gets the same ip as that of the rbd client ip that is present in the rbd status/watchers.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "2025-09-11 16:37:31.434476 D | exec: Running command: ceph rbd task add trash remove my-pool/my-namespace/cf64122559b990 --connect-timeout=15 --cluster=rook-ceph --conf=/var/lib/rook/rook-ceph/rook-ceph.config --name=client.admin --keyring=/var/lib/rook/rook-ceph/client.admin.keyring --format json\n2025-09-11 16:37:31.785810 E | cleanup: failed to add task to remove image \"csi-vol-456f3d06-cbf2-44ee-bb7b-c3fa01d3a884\" from trash in cephblockpool \"my-pool\" in rados namespace \"my-namespace\": failed to delete image \"cf64122559b990\" in cephblockpool \"my-pool\" from trash: exit status 11"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
